intro: Cyrtocymura cincta, also known as the cincte daisy, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including the potential treatment of wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cyrtocymura cincta include sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Cyrtocymura cincta is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in various folk remedies, such as infusions for fever relief and poultices for wound healing.
